name: Avl.Dict

unions.name: Dict
unions.comment: A dictionary from keys to values

values.name: empty
values.comment: The empty dictionary.
values.type: Dict k v

values.name: eq
values.comment: Tests if two dictionaries contain the same key-value pairs.
values.type: Eq k -> Eq v -> Dict k v -> Dict k v -> Bool

values.name: foldl
values.comment:
Folds the given function over the dictionary's key-value pairs,
starting with the lowest-valued key and ending with the highest-valued one.
values.type: (k -> v -> b -> b) -> b -> Dict k v -> b

values.name: foldr
values.comment:
Folds the given function over the dictionary's key-value pairs,
starting with the highest-valued key and ending with the lowest-valued one.
values.type: (k -> v -> b -> b) -> b -> Dict k v -> b

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Constructs a new dictionary from a list of key-value pairs.
values.type: Cmp k -> List ( k, v ) -> Dict k v

values.name: get
values.comment:
Returns the value associated with the key. If the key isn't in
the dictionary, `Nothing` is returned.
values.type: Cmp k -> k -> Dict k v -> Maybe v

values.name: insert
values.comment:
Inserts a key-value mapping. If the key is already in the
dictionary, the mapping is updated with the given value.
values.type: Cmp k -> k -> v -> Dict k v -> Dict k v

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment:
`True` if the dictionary is empty (i.e., maps no keys),
False` otherwise.
values.type: Dict k v -> Bool

values.name: keys
values.comment:
Returns a list of all the keys in the dictionary, in order from lowest
to highest.
values.type: Dict k v -> List k

values.name: member
values.comment: `True` if the key is in the dictionary, `False` otherwise.
values.type: Cmp k -> k -> Dict k v -> Bool

values.name: remove
values.comment:
Removes a key-value mapping. If the key is not in the dictionary,
then the dictionary is unchanged.
values.type: Cmp k -> k -> Dict k v -> Dict k v

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Creates a dictionary with one key-value mapping.
values.type: k -> v -> Dict k v

values.name: size
values.comment: The number of keys mapped by the dictionary.
values.type: Dict k v -> Int

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Returns a list of all the key-value pairs in the dictionary, in order
from lowest to highest by key.
values.type: Dict k v -> List ( k, v )

values.name: update
values.comment:
Updates a key-value mapping using the given function. If the function
returns `Nothing`, then any existing mapping for the given key will be removed.
values.type: Cmp k -> k -> (Maybe v -> Maybe v) -> Dict k v -> Dict k v

values.name: values
values.comment:
Returns a list of all the values in the dictionary, in order of their
corresponding keys, from lowest to highest.
values.type: Dict k v -> List v

name: Avl.Set

values.name: difference
values.comment: Constructs the set difference of the given sets.
values.type: Cmp k -> Set k -> Set k -> Set k

values.name: empty
values.comment: The empty set.
values.type: Set k

values.name: eq
values.comment: Tests if two sets contain the same elements.
values.type: Eq k -> Set k -> Set k -> Bool

values.name: foldl
values.comment:
Folds the given function over the elements of the set, in order from
the least element to the greatest.
values.type: (k -> b -> b) -> b -> Set k -> b

values.name: foldr
values.comment:
Folds the given function over the elements of the set, in order from
the greatest element to the least.
values.type: (k -> b -> b) -> b -> Set k -> b

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Constructs a set from a list of elements.
values.type: Cmp k -> List k -> Set k

values.name: insert
values.comment: Inserts an element into the set.
values.type: Cmp k -> k -> Set k -> Set k

values.name: intersect
values.comment: Constructs the intersection of the given sets.
values.type: Cmp k -> Set k -> Set k -> Set k

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment: `True` if the set is empty, `False` otherwise.
values.type: Set k -> Bool

values.name: member
values.comment: `True` if the given element is in the set, `False` otherwise.
values.type: Cmp k -> k -> Set k -> Bool

values.name: remove
values.comment: Removes an element from the set.
values.type: Cmp k -> k -> Set k -> Set k

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Constructs a set with a single element.
values.type: k -> Set k

values.name: size
values.comment: The size (or cardinality) of the set.
values.type: Set k -> Int

values.name: subset
values.comment:
`True` if the first set is a subset of the second,
`False` otherwise.
values.type: Cmp k -> Set k -> Set k -> Bool

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Returns a list containing all of the set's elements, in order from
least to greatest.
values.type: Set k -> List k

values.name: union
values.comment: Constructs the union of the given sets.
values.type: Cmp k -> Set k -> Set k -> Set k

name: Avl
comment:
AVL tree-based collections.

Unlike the elm-core `Dict` and `Set` types, the collections
defined in this package can be used with any type of key,
since they require the user to provide an explicit comparison
function.

The obvious downside of this approach is that the user must be
vigilant to always use the *same* comparison function on the same
data structure.

The upside, of course, is that you are not limited to using
`comparable` types as keys.

(The implementation is based on Blelloch, Ferizovic, and Sun:
"[Just Join for Parallel Ordered Sets](https://www.cs.cmu.edu/~guyb/papers/BFS16.pdf)," SPAA 2016.)

# Comparison
@docs Cmp

# Equality
@docs Eq

# Utilities
@docs cmpToEq

aliases.name: Cmp
aliases.comment: The type of comparison functions over arbitrary key types.

aliases.name: Eq
aliases.comment:
The type of equality predicates over arbitrary value types (only
used for equality tests over dictionaries and sets).

values.name: cmpToEq
values.comment:
Utility that weakens a comparison function into an equality
predicate.
values.type: Cmp k -> Eq k
